ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Hatenna via PokemonTCG.io API card swsh2-83; National Pokedex no. 856, Psychic-type emotion-sensing Pokemon introduced in Generation VIII (Pokemon Sword & Shield).

trivia_bullets.text: Hatenna (National Pokedex no. 856) is a pure Psychic-type introduced in Generation VIII. It evolves into Hattrem (no. 857) at level 32, then into Hatterene (no. 858, Psychic/Fairy) at level 42. The full line was introduced in Sword & Shield alongside the Impidimp and Milcery lines as part of Generation VIII's Fairy-type expansion.
sources: WebSearch: TheGamer 'How to Find & Evolve Hatenna into Hatterene'

trivia_bullets.text: Hatenna's line is built around a witch aesthetic: Hatterene has long layered hair draped like a cloak, and Hattrem's protruding head-hat is made of its own hair — referencing folklore belief that a witch's power is connected to her hair. The emotion-sensing protrusion is the design anchor; the species grows reclusive from emotional stimulus overload.

"Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures' emotions. If you don't have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you." The calm-disposition gating carries through to Hatterene, which will attack anything expressing strong emotions — inverting the typical friendly-Psychic-Pokemon trope.

Hatterene's signature hidden ability is Magic Bounce, which reflects status moves back at the opponent — making the final evolution a competitive anti-setup wall against Defog, Spore, and entry hazards.
